steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The blank verb needs to agree with its subject: “her book's musings on tenth-century Japanese courtly life” The head noun “musings” is plural.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. The subject is "her book's musings on tenth-century Japanese courtly life" — the head noun is the plural "musings." Rule: intervening prepositional phrases do not change the number of the head noun.
  • Choice A — ‘has fascinated’: Singular perfect — treats "musings" as singular. Agreement error. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘fascinate’: Plural simple present. Correctly agrees with the plural head noun "musings" and states a general, ongoing truth. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘is fascinating’: Singular progressive — wrong number and implies a temporary ongoing action rather than a standing literary truth. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘fascinates’: Singular simple present — wrong number for the plural subject. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
